description_long: This object is a retro-style computer monitor with a boxy, angular design reminiscent of early personal computers. The front features a large, slightly recessed rectangular screen with a thick, uniform bezel and a single green command prompt symbol in the upper left corner, evoking a classic terminal interface. Below the screen, the monitor sits atop a wide, rectangular base that includes a keyboard and a separate, blocky peripheral, likely a floppy disk drive or similar accessory. The sides and rear of the monitor are solid and unadorned, with sharp edges and a pronounced tapering from the back to the front. The entire unit is rendered in a uniform gray color, giving it a utilitarian, industrial appearance. No visible buttons, ports, or branding are present on any side of the monitor.
description: A retro, boxy computer monitor with a thick bezel, large gray screen, and integrated keyboard and peripheral, all in a uniform gray color.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large screen with a green prompt, thick bezel, and the full keyboard and peripheral layout.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the monitor's angular taper and the blocky, protruding base with attached peripheral.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, featureless back and the overall rectangular shape of the monitor and base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the monitor's depth, the keyboard, and the attached peripheral from a different angle.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and uniform on all sides, with sharp corners and a matte gray plastic appearance. There are no decorative elements or color contrasts.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, rectangular base that integrates a keyboard and a separate blocky peripheral, all forming a single, solid unit with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
